title: When Considering a New Innovation: - Ask Tough Questions: What Are Its Weaknesses
subtitle: Look Past the Hype
description:
When considering a new innovation:
- Ask tough questions: What are its weaknesses? Who benefits most from it?
- Balance optimism: Imagine both best- and worst-case scenarios.
- Seek feedback: Consult others with diverse perspectives before deciding.

Example: Excited about a new productivity app? Research reviews, ask peers, and test if it truly adds value to your workflow.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key metrics such as minutes saved per session and number of unresolved items. Measure current performance to establish a baseline for comparison.

howTo.title: Create a 7-day micro-trial plan
howTo.summary: Design a focused experiment to test the innovation. Specify the workflow, metrics to track, and success thresholds to evaluate effectiveness.

howTo.title: Conduct stakeholder interviews
howTo.summary: Engage with key stakeholders to gather insights and identify potential biases. Use their feedback to refine your approach before the trial.

howTo.title: Run the 7-day experiment
howTo.summary: Implement the micro-trial, collecting data on the defined metrics. Ensure to log session lengths, pros, and cons for thorough analysis.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track progress and make informed decisions based on the trial outcomes. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/innovation-due-diligence-checklist/.
